title: Man
content:
In the old air
        by his rocker,
        a silent trapeze of thought
        suspends an aging man.

        Each movement as of the katydid
        droning -
        a monologue with the past;
        a buzz escaping across
        still, warm air.
        Elsewhere, cicadas whittle about the octogenarian heat.

        Nestled quietly, a supine stare erodes both time & place
        unto bearded grey -
        nuances clasped
        in a breathless chat with death.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Paul Cameron Brown delivers a powerful performance in "Man"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
